title: Pararistolochia ju-ju
taxonomy: Pararistolochia ju-ju is a species of flowering plant in the family Aristolochiaceae. This genus, Aristolochia, is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as birthwort. The Aristolochiaceae family consists of 12 genera and approximately 480 species of flowering plants. These plants are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions, with a few species found in temperate regions. The classification of Pararistolochia ju-ju places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.

side_effects: Pararistolochia ju-ju, also known as the bloodroot plant, contains toxic alkaloids that can cause severe side effects when ingested. intro: Pararistolochia juju, also known as the "Juju plant," is a species of flowering plant in the Aristolochiaceae family native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its antiseptic and antibacterial effects. The bioactive constituents of Pararistolochia juju include aristolochic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, anticancer, and antiviral activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or dried and made into a powder for use in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules.
